title: Heavy rains flood some South Coast roads, trapping cars
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Local News Torrential rains impacted roadways in areas like Fall River earlier Tuesday.
Vehicles were stuck during flash flooding early Tuesday on Pleasant Street in Fall River.
The city received over 3 inches of rain since Monday.

title: Bridgewater man arrested after allegedly posing as teen, exposing himself to underage girls
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Crime The 20-year-old is accused of victimizing several girls ages 12 and 13.
A Bridgewater man has been arrested after an investigation revealed he allegedly engaged in inappropriate communications with minors, authorities said.

Orate Kyle Graham, 20, was taken into custody Thursday and is facing two counts of disseminating obscene material to a minor and one count of accosting or annoying another person, the Bridgewater Police Department said in a press release.

title: No evidence of tumor cluster at Newton-Wellesley Hospital after 11 reported tumors in nurses, external review finds
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Local News The review found that the hospital's facility did not cause the tumors.

Newton-Wellesley Hospital Pat Greenhouse/Globe Staff An external review concluded there is no evidence of a workplace tumor cluster at Newton-Wellesley Hospital after 11 nurses working on the same floor reported being diagnosed with brain tumors.

According to the report published March 30, Stefanos Kales, a professor of medicine at Harvard Medical School, concluded that “the overall hospital environment is safe” and that the reported cases do not appear to represent a true workplace tumor cluster.
